raw_consumer_statement:
The fraudulent charge occurred on or about XX/XX/XXXX Amex reversed my valid dispute and reinstated the {$2600.00} charge in XX/XX/XXXX I am the victim of a well-documented XXXX scam by XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX, NY ) XXXX 

On or about XX/XX/XXXX, a telemarketer called me and spent over XXXX hour flattering me and stating that inclusion in their directory was free and merit-based. 

Only at the very end did they switch to high-pressure sales, demanding {$2600.00} for a lifetime membership + plaque and claiming the one-day-only price would expire that night if I didnt give my card immediately. 

I felt coerced and provided my American Express card. The {$2600.00} charge posted the next day. 

I disputed the charge immediately with American Express. 

Amex initially sided with me and reversed the charge, correctly recognizing it as fraud. 
However, after XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX submitted rebuttal documents ( likely a scripted recording or order form obtained under deception ), Amex reversed their decision and reinstated the full {$2600.00} charge to my account without adequately investigating the merchants long history of identical scams. 

This merchant has : A XXXX XXXX XXXX rating with dozens of complaints describing the exact same XXXX minute flattery sudden {$2600.00} hard-close script Been labeled a XXXX scam by XXXX, XXXX, and hundreds XXXX consumer reports Uses tactics that violate the federal Telemarketing Sales Rule ( 16 CFR 310.3 misrepresentation and artificial urgency ) I have already submitted a detailed appeal to American Express with : A signed affidavit/timeline of the fraudulent call XXXX  complaint screenshots XXXX XXXX XXXX scam article FTC XXXX report # XXXX State Attorney General complaint ( filed XX/XX/XXXX ) American Express is failing in its XXXX XXXX protect cardholders from known fraudulent merchants by siding with XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX despite overwhelming evidence XXXX deception. 

I request that the CFPB require American Express to : Permanently remove the {$2600.00} charge Issue a full credit to my account Provide a written explanation of why they initially reversed the charge, then reinstated it against clear evidence of fraud
